# Results Registry

## entropy_minimal_5_5

- **Value:** 1.0 discovery_rate
- **Description:** Log-precision fitness, minimal terminals {k,1,-1,2}, no injection: 5/5 seeds found Leibniz
- **Interpretation:** Log-precision fitness with minimal terminal set {k,1,-1,2} and no injection achieves 100% Leibniz discovery — the strongest v3 result, showing log-precision fitness is sufficient even from the most constrained search space
- **Methodology:** 5 seeds (42,7,137,2718,31415), 30-min total budget, 360s/seed; fraction of seeds whose best expression is the Leibniz series or equivalent; from entropy_leibniz_v3_minimal.py TEVV report
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** entropy_leibniz_v3_minimal.py
- **Computed From:** entropy_v3_minimal_results

## entropy_minimal_runtime

- **Value:** 369.9 seconds
- **Description:** Log-precision v3 minimal total runtime for 5/5 discovery (all seeds)
- **Interpretation:** Log-precision fitness discovers Leibniz in all 5 seeds within 369.9 seconds total — well under the 1800s budget, indicating log-precision fitness converges efficiently once on the right track
- **Methodology:** Sum of per-seed elapsed times from entropy_leibniz_v3_minimal.py TEVV report; 5 seeds, up to 360s each, 1800s total budget
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** entropy_leibniz_v3_minimal.py
- **Computed From:** entropy_v3_minimal_results

## entropy_stress_l1_0_5

- **Value:** 0.0 discovery_rate
- **Description:** Log-precision fitness fails at 15 terminals (0/5). Wrong-limit attractor 5/((6+4k)(k-2)) scores ti=15.93 > Leibniz 15.29
- **Interpretation:** Log-precision fitness fails completely at Level 1 (15 terminals): 0/5 seeds discover Leibniz because wrong-limit attractors score higher than Leibniz under the baseline fitness; the fitness landscape is deceptive at this terminal set size
- **Methodology:** entropy_stress_test.py --level 1; 5 seeds, 30-min budget; 0 seeds found a Leibniz-equivalent expression; Level 1 config: TERM_FIXED=[k,1,-1,2] + EPHEMERALS=[-5..5]
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** entropy_stress_test.py
- **Computed From:** entropy_stress_L1_data

## fitness_largeT_w0_1

- **Value:** 0.2 discovery_rate
- **Description:** Fitness sensitivity: large-T penalty w=0.1 on 15-terminal entropy: 1/5. Only fix that found Leibniz (seed 137, 389 gens)
- **Interpretation:** The large-T penalty approach (penalizing high error at T=10000) is the only fitness fix that recovers any Level 1 discovery: 1/5 seeds (seed 137) found Leibniz in 389 generations; the other two approaches (extended checkpoints, rate-consistency variance) failed entirely
- **Methodology:** fitness_sensitivity_test.py --approach 2 --weight 0.1; Level 1 config (15 terminals); 5 seeds; result from fitness_sensitivity_results.md
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** fitness_sensitivity_test.py
- **Computed From:** fitness_approach2_w01_data

## gp_alpha_0_5_4_5

- **Value:** 0.8 discovery_rate
- **Description:** GP v3 minimal with alpha=0.5 (convergence bonus weight): 4/5 seeds found Leibniz
- **Interpretation:** Raising the convergence bonus weight from alpha=0.05 to alpha=0.5 improves GP discovery from 2/5 to 4/5 seeds, but not to 5/5; convergence bonus alone cannot fully compensate for insufficient population diversity at pop=1000
- **Methodology:** gp_sensitivity_sweep.py --alpha 0.5; 5 seeds, pop=1000, minimal terminal set; reported as fraction of seeds finding Leibniz-equivalent expression
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** gp_sensitivity_sweep.py
- **Computed From:** gp_sensitivity_sweep_data

## gp_minimal_2_5

- **Value:** 0.4 discovery_rate
- **Description:** GP convergence fitness, minimal terminals, no injection, pop=1000: 2/5 seeds found Leibniz
- **Interpretation:** GP convergence fitness at pop=1000 with minimal terminals discovers Leibniz in only 2/5 seeds — insufficient reliability, motivating the population phase-transition investigation
- **Methodology:** 5 seeds (42,7,137,2718,31415), 30-min budget, 360s/seed, pop=1000; fraction of seeds whose best expression converges to pi/4; from gp_leibniz_v3_minimal.py TEVV report
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** gp_leibniz_v3_minimal.py
- **Computed From:** gp_v3_minimal_convergence

## gp_pop2000_5_5

- **Value:** 1.0 discovery_rate
- **Description:** GP convergence fitness, minimal terminals, no injection, pop=2000: 5/5 seeds. Phase transition at pop=2000.
- **Interpretation:** Doubling population to 2000 triggers a phase transition for GP convergence fitness: reliability jumps from 2/5 to 5/5, suggesting a critical mass threshold below which diversity collapses
- **Methodology:** 5 seeds (42,7,137,2718,31415), 30-min budget, 360s/seed, pop=2000; gp_sensitivity_sweep.py --pop_size 2000; fraction of seeds finding Leibniz
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** gp_sensitivity_sweep.py
- **Computed From:** gp_sensitivity_sweep_data

## info_rate_3_32

- **Value:** 3.32 bits_per_decade
- **Description:** Constant precision gain rate for all discovered Leibniz expressions. Theoretical rate from error bound 1/(2T+1).
- **Interpretation:** Every discovered Leibniz-equivalent expression exhibits the same 3.32 bits/decade precision gain rate, matching the theoretical prediction from the error bound 1/(2T+1); this universality confirms the log-precision fitness selects for second-order convergence structure
- **Methodology:** Slope of log2(1/|partial_sum(T) - pi/4|) vs log10(T) over T checkpoints for all 5 discovered expressions; theoretical value = log2(10) ≈ 3.32 bits/decade from Leibniz error bound 1/(2T+1)
- **State:** verified
- **Derived From:** leibniz_error_bound_derivation

## leibniz_ti_15_29

- **Value:** 15.29 bits
- **Description:** Leibniz series info(T=10000) = -log2(|error|) = 15.29 bits
- **Interpretation:** Leibniz achieves 15.29 bits of precision at T=10000, which is lower than the wrong-limit attractor (15.93 bits); this explains why the baseline log-precision fitness preferentially selects wrong-limit attractors at Level 1
- **Methodology:** Computed as -log2(|sum_{k=0}^{9999} (-1)^k/(2k+1) - pi/4|); Leibniz error at T=10000 is approximately 1/20001
- **State:** verified
- **Derived From:** leibniz_error_bound_derivation

## v2_confounded_5_5

- **Value:** 1.0 discovery_rate
- **Description:** GP v2 and Entropy v2 5/5 results were CONFOUNDED: Leibniz tree injected at gen 0, survived via elitism. Not discovered.
- **Interpretation:** The 5/5 discovery rates reported for GP v2 and Entropy v2 are NOT evidence of autonomous discovery: Leibniz was injected into the initial population at gen 0 and preserved via elitism; all v2 results must be interpreted as recognition tests only
- **Methodology:** Retrospective audit: gp_leibniz_v2.py and entropy_leibniz.py both include Leibniz injection in population initialization; this was identified after v3 was designed to remove injection; v2 confound documented in CLAUDE.md Known Issues
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** gp_leibniz_v2.py

## wrong_limit_ti_15_93

- **Value:** 15.93 bits
- **Description:** Wrong-limit attractor 5/((6+4k)(k-2)) achieves 15.93 bits at T=10000, exceeding Leibniz (15.29). Converges to ~0.7855 != pi/4.
- **Interpretation:** The wrong-limit attractor 5/((6+4k)(k-2)) achieves higher log-precision fitness than Leibniz within the T=10000 evaluation window despite converging to ~0.7855 instead of pi/4; this is the root cause of Level 1 failure and motivates the large-T penalty fitness fix
- **Methodology:** Manually evaluated: computed partial sums up to T=10000, computed info=-log2(|sum-pi/4|); identified during fitness_sensitivity_test.py analysis as the dominant attractor at Level 1
- **State:** verified
- **Generated By:** entropy_stress_test.py
- **Computed From:** entropy_stress_L1_data
